description: ED-<LANE>-NNNN lane-tagged editorial namespace created (Jordan). Motivated by TWO same-session concurrent-allocation collisions on the flat ED-NNNN sequence within one PR (#58): first ED-1088 collided with an independently-merged mass-battle ED-1088 (LC-8, PR #59), renumbered to ED-1090; then ED-1089/1090 collided AGAIN with three more mass-battle EDs independently landed via PR #62/#65, renumbered to ED-1093/1094 (see ED-1094's own entry for the full incident). The existing references/id_reservations.yaml A-E block scheme only prevents collision through allocation DISCIPLINE (read next_free before allocating, retry on races); two same-session collisions on one active block in a single PR is a real signal that flat-numeric blocks alone do not scale to genuinely concurrent multi-session work on this repo. RULING (Jordan): new EDs use ED-<LANE>-NNNN, a 2-letter lane code + 4-digit zero-padded sequence, making cross-lane collision impossible BY CONSTRUCTION rather than by retryable discipline. Lane roster (9): MB mass battle, PC personal combat, FI field investigation, SC social contest, FA faction actions, WR world, IN infrastructure/cross-cutting, GO godot, SE settlements -- SC (social contest) and PC (personal/scene combat) were deliberately disambiguated after the first draft proposed SC for both; PY (python) was proposed and then dropped as not a real lane (a language, not a subsystem). MIGRATION: new-IDs-only -- the flat ED-NNNN sequence is FROZEN as of this entry (verified_live_max.ED=1094, no new flat allocations) but remains permanently valid; none of the existing ~1094 entries are retrofitted. Both formats resolve through the same ledger/citation-audit path forever. IMPLEMENTATION: references/id_reservations.yaml gained a lane_ids section (per-lane next_free counters, all starting at 1 except IN which starts at 2 -- this entry is ED-IN-0001, the cutover's own first-ever lane-tagged id, allocated to itself); tools/validate_ed_citations.py's CITE_RE/build_status_map/_walk_entries extended to recognize ED-<LANE>-NNNN alongside flat ED-NNNN (both fold through the same _canon_id normalization, zero-padding-insensitive); tools/currency_consistency_check.py gained check_lane_id_ceilings (per-lane ceiling drift, mirroring the existing flat check_id_ceilings, deliberately independent since the flat ceiling is now frozen); tools/atomizer.py and tools/index_gen.py flagged (not fixed -- low priority, atomizer's archival chunking won't hit lane-tagged volume for a long time; index_gen is already SUPERSEDED/dead) with known-gap comments rather than silent breakage. CLAUDE.md Section 3 documents the format contract and a NEW, NOT YET CI-ENFORCED convention: a session should declare its lane (via the ED-<LANE> ids it allocates) and keep commits/PRs scoped to that lane's files, except genuinely cross-cutting IN work or cross-lane-collision resolution. Mechanical enforcement of the scoping rule (detecting which lane a PR's file changes belong to and flagging mismatches) is explicitly NOT built here -- flagged as real follow-up work, not invented as a fuzzy heuristic gate under time pressure. Tests: 5 new cases in tests/hooks/test_ed_citation_integrity.py (lane-tagged parsing, padding, NONEXISTENT/OPEN_AS_BASIS, flat/lane-tagged non-collision, unrecognized-lane-code non-match) + 4 new cases in tests/valoria/test_currency_consistency_check.py (lane-max extraction ignoring flat ids, ceiling drift, missing-lane flagging, clean-when-empty) -- all green alongside the full existing suite.

description: valoria-dice-model: added the canonical continuous (Godot-mode) resolver alongside the existing legacy-TTRPG discrete d10 model, per Jordan's confirmation via AskUserQuestion. Prior state: valoria_dice.py implemented only the discrete pool model and the SKILL.md presented it flatly as 'Current standard' with no acknowledgment that params/core.md section Continuous Engine (Decision E, 2026-05-15) designates net ~ Normal(mu*N, sigma*sqrt(N)) as canonical for the videogame/Godot implementation specifically, with the discrete rule demoted to legacy/TTRPG-mode only. Added continuous_outcome_probs(n, tn, ob) and continuous_quick_check(n, tn, ob) to valoria_dice.py -- closed-form via the normal CDF (math.erf, no scipy dependency), continuity-corrected (resolves against x - 0.5 per the ER-2 fix, params/core.md commit a3d3888) so odds track the discrete model at small pools, matching params/core.md's own Degrees of Success thresholds (Overwhelming net >= max(2*Ob,3), Success net >= Ob, Partial 0<net<Ob, Failure net<=0) and supporting fractional Ob (canonical in videogame mode). Validated by direct comparison against the existing discrete Monte Carlo implementation at n=4/8/12, TN7, Ob3: continuous p_full within 0.003-0.008 of discrete across all three pool sizes, consistent with params/core.md's own claimed max-deviation-0.029 equivalence. SKILL.md rewritten with a 'Two Resolver Modes' section framing which mode governs which context (TTRPG-facing vs. Godot-facing questions), a new Task 9 (continuous-mode table), and removal of a dangling params/combat.md row from the Params File Reference table (that file does not exist, deprecated 2026-06-04).

description: Repo-wide sweep of every engine/resolver/simulator/test/Python surface (sim/ 104 files, tests/ 206 files across tests/valoria [real, CI-gated, 141 passed], sim/tests [real ED-1053 regression oracle, 419 passed], tests/contracts [34 passed], tests/sim + tests/sim_framework [both dead/archival despite the name collision with sim/ -- disambiguated per sim/README.md], plus ~2.5MB of non-executable narrative-audit markdown; tools/ 44 files; the 8 audit/simulation skills) to evaluate simulation/testing methodology and propose a harness. Builds directly on PR #122's audit-ecosystem consolidation (ED-IN-0032..0037, merged 2026-07-11), which fixed the audit-TOOLING layer but explicitly left the audit_registry.jsonl live-logging gap and any simulation-execution harness untouched (see ED-IN-0035). Findings: contract_adjudicator.py (the one real A1-A12 contract-closure linter) is unit-tested but never run against the live module_contracts.yaml by CI; combat_sim.py/valoria_dice.py produce real Monte Carlo numbers with no asserted CI thresholds, only human prose judgment; 5 of 8 audit skills have zero executable backing; audit_registry.jsonl has exactly 5 entries, all backfilled, none ever live-appended by an actual skill run despite every skill claiming to; tests/hooks/, tests/index/, tests/registry/, and 2 files under tests/sim/ contain real pytest code no CI job or local hook executes; sim/provincial/massbattle.py (1905 LOC, CANONICAL) has zero dedicated unit test; sim/personal/combat.py is confirmed dead (DEPRECATED banner, 2026-06-23) but remains importable. Proposal filed at designs/audit/2026-07-12-simulation-test-harness-methodology/simulation_test_harness_methodology_v1.md (PROPOSED): a single generic harness core (canon-parameter resolver bound to CURRENT.md, never fabricates) plus one thin per-module Adapter (the modular 'test module' requested) bound to module_contracts.yaml's existing IN->resolver->OUT shape rather than a new interface; a depth-tiered (1 minor/2 medium/3 major) probabilistic branch-exploration policy per resolver-call 'event'; and a mandatory triage-flag taxonomy (CANON_GAP/CONTRACT_VIOLATION/OUT_OF_RANGE/STUB_HIT/DEGENERATE_DISTRIBUTION/FABRICATION_RISK) that can never be silently swallowed into a PASS verdict. A minimal Gate-0 prototype ships alongside the doc at tools/sim_harness/ (harness core + logger + one demo adapter over valoria_dice.py, deliberately the simplest, lowest-game-balance-risk resolver in the repo) -- confirmed runnable, and its own audit_registry.jsonl append in this same commit is the first LIVE (non-backfilled) entry the registry has ever received, concretely demonstrating the fix rather than only proposing it. Rollout order (design doc section 8): dice model -> combat_sim -> social contest kernel -> mass battle -> full mc_v18 campaigns, each wave report-only in CI before promotion to blocking, mirroring PR #122's own ratchet pattern. Four independent quick-win findings (section 9) are cheap and separable from the architecture decision: wire or retire the three orphaned real-pytest directories; guard against sim/personal/combat.py accidental reimport; retire tools/propagator.py/find_references.py/verify_cuts.py (same orphaned-tool profile as the 2026-07-09 batch, missed by that sweep's exact heuristics); wire contract_adjudicator.py into CI report-only independent of the rest of this proposal. NEEDS JORDAN (called out per CLAUDE.md section 2's loud-exception rule, not routine work to ratify on merge): the section-8 rollout order (mass battle has the largest raw coverage gap but is deliberately sequenced fourth, not first); whether wave 1's CI job must go through the full report-only burn-in every other checker used or can block sooner; whether a full-campaign mc_v18 harness run should ever gate a PR given its cost/blast-radius; and whether the four quick wins should land in this same PR or be filed as separate lane work.

resolution: RATIFIED 2026-07-12. The 4 open questions (design doc section 11) were put to Jordan directly via AskUserQuestion, not assumed. Answers: (1) rollout order -- Jordan flagged a real gap ("Where is settlement management, faction actions, field investigations, threadwork?"); section 8 extended to add faction_action.py, sim/territory/*, and sim/thread/* as waves 5-7 (mass battle stays wave 4, campaign composition now wave 8); field investigation explicitly excluded, not omitted -- its sim/ implementation is still [PROVISIONAL] stub-only, no real resolver to adapt yet. (2) Wave 1 CI burn-in: "Full report-only burn-in" -- existing ratchet convention applies, no deviation. (3) mc_v18.py as CI gate: "Never gate a PR" -- firm constraint on the eventual campaign adapter. (4) four section-9 quick-win findings: "File separately" -- filed as ED-IN-0045 rather than bundled into this PR. Between initial filing and ratification, the Gate-0 prototype went through six rounds of adversarial review + deliberate stress-testing (not part of the original filing) that found and fixed 34 real bugs -- see tools/sim_harness/README.md for the full account. PR jordanelias/ttrpg#124. COLLISION 2026-07-12, resolved by renumbering the later-merging side (same pattern documented elsewhere in this ledger): this PR (#124, branch claude/repo-audit-testing-methodology-w2f1wo) originally allocated ED-IN-0038/0039 for the simulation-harness methodology + prototype, but a separate PR (#126, "Skills-ecosystem staleness remediation, Phase 7") independently claimed ED-IN-0038 through ED-IN-0043 and merged to main first. Renumbered ED-IN-0038 -> ED-IN-0044 (this entry) and ED-IN-0039 -> ED-IN-0045 (the quick-wins follow-up) during the merge reconciling onto main.
